Arguments.prompt: Generate a dataset with three numeric variables: temperature, humidity, and pressure. Each variable should contain 1000 random values. Temperature should be normally distributed around 20 with std 5 (in Celsius). Humidity should be uniformly distributed between 20 and 100 (percent). Pressure should be normally distributed around 1013 with std 10 (hPa). Save the dataset as a single file where each key is a variable name and the value is a list of numbers.

Read the file(s) produced by the upstream action. For each of the three variables (temperature, humidity, pressure), compute the mean and standard deviation. Save the results as a file with the structure: {variable_name: {mean: ..., std: ...}} for each variable.

Read the file(s) produced by upstream actions. Create a figure with three subplots (one per variable: temperature, humidity, pressure). Each subplot should be a histogram of the variable's values with a vertical line marking the mean and a shaded region indicating +/- one standard deviation. Label axes clearly and include a title for each subplot. Save the figure as plots.png using matplotlib (use the Agg backend: matplotlib.use('Agg') before importing pyplot).
